userStories.title: Add AcceptanceFixConfig to config schema with defaults and DiagnosisResult type
userStories.description: Add AcceptanceFixConfig interface to src/config/types.ts following the existing pattern for acceptance config. Fields: diagnoseModel (string, default 'fast'), fixModel (string, default 'balanced'), strategy ('diagnose-first' | 'implement-only', default 'diagnose-first'), maxRetries (number, default 2). Wire into NaxConfig.acceptance.fix in the config type. Add defaults to src/config/defaults.ts. Add DiagnosisResult interface to src/acceptance/types.ts with fields: verdict ('source_bug' | 'test_bug' | 'both'), reasoning (string), confidence (number 0-1), testIssues (optional string[]), sourceIssues (optional string[]). Add validation in src/config/schema.ts to reject unknown strategy values.
acceptanceCriteria: NaxConfig.acceptance.fix has type AcceptanceFixConfig with fields diagnoseModel (string), fixModel (string), strategy ('diagnose-first' | 'implement-only'), maxRetries (number)
DEFAULT_CONFIG.acceptance.fix equals { diagnoseModel: 'fast', fixModel: 'balanced', strategy: 'diagnose-first', maxRetries: 2 }
DiagnosisResult interface exported from src/acceptance/types.ts with required fields verdict, reasoning, confidence and optional fields testIssues, sourceIssues
Config validation rejects strategy values other than 'diagnose-first' and 'implement-only'

routing.reasoning: Type definitions and config defaults only — purely additive

userStories.title: Implement diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() as full agent session with correct ACP session naming
userStories.description: Create src/acceptance/fix-diagnosis.ts. Implement diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() that runs a full agent session via adapter.run() (NOT adapter.complete()) with sessionRole 'diagnose'. Session name follows pattern nax-<hash>-<feature>-<storyId>-diagnose via buildSessionName(). The prompt should include: failing test output (truncated to 2000 chars), full acceptance test file content, and auto-detected source file contents (parse import statements from test file, read up to 5 files, 500 lines each). Parse DiagnosisResult JSON from agent output. On parse failure, return { verdict: 'source_bug', reasoning: 'diagnosis failed', confidence: 0 }. Model resolved from config.acceptance.fix.diagnoseModel via resolveModelForAgent(). When protocol is ACP, the session must appear in acpx list with correct session name.
acceptanceCriteria: diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() receives the agent adapter via parameter (from ctx.agentGetFn in caller) — never calls bare getAgent()
diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() calls agent.run() (not agent.complete()) with sessionRole 'diagnose'
Session name follows pattern nax-<hash>-<feature>-<storyId>-diagnose via buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, 'diagnose')
diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() resolves diagnoseModel tier from config.acceptance.fix.diagnoseModel via resolveModelForAgent() — never passes raw tier name
diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() auto-detects source file paths from import statements in the test file content and includes their content in the prompt (up to 5 files, 500 lines each)
When agent output contains valid JSON matching DiagnosisResult schema, diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() returns the parsed result
When agent output is invalid or unparsable, diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() returns { verdict: 'source_bug', reasoning: 'diagnosis failed — falling back to source fix', confidence: 0 }
When adapter.run() throws an error, diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() catches it and returns the fallback DiagnosisResult
When config.agent.protocol is 'acp', the diagnosis session runs as a proper ACP session visible in acpx list

routing.reasoning: New module with agent session integration, import parsing, ACP protocol handling — moderate complexity

userStories.title: Implement executeSourceFix() as full agent session with correct ACP session naming
userStories.description: Add executeSourceFix() to src/acceptance/fix-executor.ts. This function calls adapter.run() directly (NOT the pipeline, NOT adapter.complete()) with sessionRole 'source-fix'. Session name follows pattern nax-<hash>-<feature>-<storyId>-source-fix via buildSessionName(). The session prompt includes: failing test output, acceptance test file path, diagnosis reasoning (if available), and instruction 'Fix the source implementation. Do NOT modify the test file.' After the session completes, verify by running bun test against the acceptance test file. Returns { success, cost }. Model resolved from config.acceptance.fix.fixModel via resolveModelForAgent(). When protocol is ACP, the session must appear in acpx list with correct session name.
acceptanceCriteria: executeSourceFix() receives the agent adapter via parameter (from ctx.agentGetFn in caller) — never calls bare getAgent()
executeSourceFix() calls agent.run() (not agent.complete(), not pipeline) with sessionRole 'source-fix'
Session name follows pattern nax-<hash>-<feature>-<storyId>-source-fix via buildSessionName(workdir, featureName, storyId, 'source-fix')
executeSourceFix() resolves fixModel tier from config.acceptance.fix.fixModel via resolveModelForAgent() — never passes raw tier name
executeSourceFix() includes failing test output and diagnosis reasoning in the agent prompt
executeSourceFix() does NOT use the pipeline — calls adapter.run() directly, then verifies by running bun test <acceptance-test-path>
executeSourceFix() returns { success: boolean, cost: number } where cost is extracted from AgentResult
When config.agent.protocol is 'acp', the fix session runs as a proper ACP session visible in acpx list with correct session name

routing.reasoning: Agent session integration with ACP protocol handling, verification step — requires understanding of adapter.run() contract

userStories.title: Wire diagnosis and fix routing into acceptance-loop.ts
userStories.description: Replace the current generateAndAddFixStories() + full pipeline flow in runAcceptanceLoop() with the new two-phase approach. When strategy is 'diagnose-first': call diagnoseAcceptanceFailure(), then route based on verdict — source_bug calls executeSourceFix(), test_bug calls regenerateAcceptanceTest(), both calls executeSourceFix() first then regenerateAcceptanceTest() if still failing. When strategy is 'implement-only': skip diagnosis, call executeSourceFix() directly. Use acceptance.fix.maxRetries for fix retry count. Emit JSONL events: acceptance.diagnosis (verdict, confidence), acceptance.source-fix (cost, success), acceptance.test-regen (outcome). Keep the existing generateAndAddFixStories() code path removable but functional during transition.
acceptanceCriteria: acceptance-loop.ts obtains agent via (ctx.agentGetFn ?? _acceptanceLoopDeps.getAgent)(agentName) and passes it to diagnoseAcceptanceFailure() and executeSourceFix() — never bare getAgent()

When strategy is 'diagnose-first' and diagnosis verdict is 'source_bug', acceptance-loop calls executeSourceFix() (which uses agent.run() with sessionRole 'source-fix') instead of generateAndAddFixStories()
When strategy is 'diagnose-first' and diagnosis verdict is 'test_bug', acceptance-loop calls regenerateAcceptanceTest() then re-runs acceptance validation
When strategy is 'diagnose-first' and diagnosis verdict is 'both', acceptance-loop calls executeSourceFix() first, then calls regenerateAcceptanceTest() if acceptance still fails
When strategy is 'implement-only', acceptance-loop skips diagnosis and calls executeSourceFix() directly
Fix retries respect config.acceptance.fix.maxRetries (separate from config.acceptance.maxRetries)
JSONL event with stage 'acceptance.diagnosis' emitted containing verdict and confidence fields
JSONL event with stage 'acceptance.source-fix' emitted containing cost and success fields
JSONL event with stage 'acceptance.test-regen' emitted containing outcome field

routing.reasoning: Complex integration replacing existing flow with verdict-based routing, multiple code paths, event emission
